{{Quote|Lankin is known to be secretive, rumored to seldom venture from his governor's palace, except for occasional hunting expeditions to the Equatorial Reserves of Xandil VII.|Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
'''Xandil VII''' was an astronomical object<ref name="WbC" /> located in the [[Xandil system/Legends|Xandil system]], a part of the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It shared its name with the [[Far Xandil sector/Legends|Far Xandil sector]], which bordered the Lambda [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].<ref name="Mid Rim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-sectors-of-the-mid-rim|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Sectors of the Mid Rim}}</ref>

''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] composed by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] as a compilation of reports on various threats to the New Republic and published<ref name="WbC" /> in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Date">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> noted the rumors that the only occasions on which [[Par Lankin]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the Lambda sector, left his [[Lankin's palace|governor's palace]] were [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] expeditions to the [[Equatorial Reserves]] of Xandil VII.<ref name="WbC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Xandil VII was mentioned in the [[1993]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], which was written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Xandil [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Xandil VII, in grid square P-16.<ref name="TEA" />
